def: n. f. S. हरिद्रा Pr. haladdā . Turmeric ( Curcuma longa ). Hardī jardī nā taje, khaṭ-ras taje na ām, Jo hardī jardī taje tau augan taje gulām . No mango is from acid free, nor haldī from its yellow hue, Did haldī e'er its color lose the slave might then his vice eschew. haldī lagā-ke baiṭhnā , v. n. To presume a great deal. haldī lagī na phaṭkarī , (Dyers) Without cost; net gain or profit. 1. IIaldī lage na phaṭkari, raṅg chokhā āve! Prov. For alum or turmeric he nothing spends, 'a clear bright dye give me,' he says. 2. Haldī lagī na phiṭkarī, paṭākh bahū ān paṛī! Prov. Not a penny for turmeric or alum spent, a daughter-in-law is in the house ensconced! (She is married without any ceremony or expense). āṅbā haldī, jaṅglī haldī , n. f. Curcumœ zedoariœ radix ; the long zedoary.

ety: Prk. हलद्दी, or हलद्दा; S. हरिद्रा
def: s.f. Turmeric, Curcuma longa

subs: s.f., or haldī ćaṛhānā, s.m. Name of a marriage-ceremony in which the bride and bridegroom are anointed with turmeric (i.q. hald-hāt, q.v.)
'To apply haldī (to the hands, &c.) and sit down'; to play the bride, or the grand lady; to give oneself airs, to presume a great deal
'No outlay for turmeric or alum'; without cost; — net gain or profit

def: s.m. corr. of next, q.v.

Making oath; swearing. halaf-nāma , n. m. A declaration upon oath; a written solemn declaration by a person exempt by the Regulations from being sworn in the ordinary manner; an affidavit. hasb-i-zābitah halaf dīyā gayā . Sworn in due form.

(-ko), To administer an oath (to), put (one) on oath
s.m. A declaration on oath, an affidavit; a written solemn declaration by a person exempt by the regulations from being sworn in the ordinary manner
adv. On oath; by an oath ( = ḥalfaṉ).

def: v.n. To heave (as the stomach); to feel nausea, to nauseate (syn. matlānā).
s.m. A division of crops in which the zamīndār gets seven, and the cultivator nine, parts.
s.m. The fauces; the throat, the gullet, the æsophagus; the windpipe
more: n. m. The wind-pipe; the throat. Halaq se niklī, khalaq meṅ paṛī . Prov. By the throat expressed, by the world possessed. halq band k. , v. a. 1. To cause one to hold his tongue; to silence. 2. To interrupt; check; stop one who is speaking. halaq baiṭhnā , v. n. To become hoarse. halaq dabānā , v. a. To throttle; stifle; smother; strangle. halaq ke darbān , Wom. One's own children. Khāne baiṭhte hī halaq ke darbān ā jāte haiṅ . No sooner I sit down to dine my throat's watch hang about these children mine. (The morsel wont go down till she has given a portion to her children who press about her). be-halaq , adj. lit . having no throat. Ravenous; greedy; covetous.
s. m. The windpipe, the throat. حلق پر چهري اچهنا ḥalḳ par chhurī achhnā (dakh. i. q. کاوش هونا) Investigation to be (?).
subs: (-kā), To make (one) hold his tongue, to silence; to stop (one) from speaking, to interrupt
(-kā), The throat be set, to become hoarse
(-kā), To press or squeeze the throat (of), to throttle, to strangle
adj. 'Having no throat'; ravenous, greedy, covetous.
wety: Borrowed from Arabic حلق (ḥalaq).

ety: for A. حلقة ḥalqat, fem.; v.n. fr. حلق 'to shave'
def: s.m. A circle, a ring, hoop, link, loop, button-hole; the collar (of harness); a company (of people), assembly, fraternity; a circle, a circuit (of a village, &c.); a boundary line which includes all the lands and dwellings of a village or hamlet; knocker (of a door); — a kind of firework
more: n. m. حلق he drew a circle. 1. A circle; circuit. 2. A boundary line which includes all the lands and dwellings of a village or hamlet. 3. A ring; loop; button-hole. 4. The collar (of harness). 5. A badge of slavery. halqa-i-ustuvānā; H. lambotrī kaṛī . A cylindric ring. halqa bāṅdhnā , v. a. To form a ring; encompass; hem in; besiege. halqa ba-gosh, lit . with the ring in the ear. A slave. halqa-bandī , n. f. 1. A concentric division of villages, etc. 2. Arrangement of villages in circles or groups for village accountants, schools, etc. maktab-i-halqa bandī , A village school supported from Local Funds.

s. m. 1. A ring, a circle, an assembly, a fraternity. 2. A kind of firework. 3. A knocker (of a door). حلقه بگوش ḥalḳa-ba-gosh, A slave, a servant: i. e. One who wears a ring in his ear, as a badge of servitude. حلقه بگوشي ḥalḳa ba-gosh-ī, s. f. Servitude, slavery. حلقه زن ḥalḳa-zan, Knocking (at a door).
subs: (-kā), To form a circle or ring; — to encompass, surround; to besiege
s.m. lit. 'Having a ring in the ear' (as a badge of servitude); a slave
s.f. Arrangement of villages in circles or groups (for village accountants, schools, &c.); a concentric division of villages, &c.; of or pertaining to such an arrangement of villages, &c.

def: adj. S. लघु light. Opp. of bhārī . See ادني and اوچها (1, 2, 4—7). 1. Light; not heavy. 2. Poor; not fertile. 3. Shallow. 4. Small. 5. Trifling. 6. Moderate; low. 7. Cheap; low-priced. Halke dāmoṅ kā . 8. Depressed (trade). 9. Easy; light. Halkā kām . 10. Ineffectual. 11. Insignificant. 12. Silly. 13. Mean; shabby. halke bhārī honā , v. n. 1. To be sick and tired of; to feel burdensome. Chār din ke vāste kyoṅ halke bhārī hote ho? 2. To lower or humiliate oneself. halkā-pan, halkā-panā , n. m. 1. Lightness. 2. Shallowness. 3. Insignificance. 4. Levity. 5. Meanness. halkā-phulkā, halkī-phulkī, halkā phūl , adj. Light as a feather. halkā raṅg , n. m. A light color. halkā k. , v. a. 1. To lighten (a load). 2. To abate; reduce; mitigate ( kam k . 1—4). 3. To lower (in one's eyes); disgrace. Unke sāmne mujhe halkā na karo! halkā khānā . Light food. halkā lahū , Thin blood. halkā mol lagānā , v. a. To put a low or moderate price upon. halkā h. , v. n. 1. To become or feel light. Nahāë se dehī halkī ho gaī . 2. To be abated, mitigated, etc. Bukhār halkā ho gayā . 3. To be freed from an obligation or responsibility. Unke bojh se halke hue . 4. To ease oneself. Halke ho lo, pīchhe chaleṅge! 5. To be ashamed, abashed. Nāhaq un se halkā honā paṛā . 6. ( Slang ) To have sexual intercourse with. kalkī bāt kahnā , To say something unworthy of oneself. halkī jamā . Light assessment. halkī choṭ . A slight hurt or wound. halkī chorī ; Ped. sarqa-i-khafīf , n. f. Petty theft. halke halke, haule haule ; Rus. halve halve ; Farrukh. haruge haruge , adv. Slowly ( sahaj meṅ , 1).

ety: Prk. हलुकअं, or लहुकअं; S. लघुक+कं
def: adj. (f. -ī), Light (in weight, or in character, or in colour, &c.); unimportant, of no consequence, of little moment; of little or low estimation, or authority, or influence; ineffectual; insignificant; frivolous, puerile; mean, pitiful, petty, scurvy, shabby, despicable, debased; silly; — small (as a measure, or capacity, &c.); weak; easy; poor (as soil, or as blood); shallow; mild, gentle, soft (as wind, heat, cold, rain, &c.); neap (tide); — faint, pale (as colour); soft (as water); — trifling, slight (matter, or work); manageable; easy of endurance; — light, easy of digestion (as food, or diet); of low price, or of little value; low, moderate; cheap; — depressed (trade)
more: adj. Light (in weight or character), debased, mean, silly, easy, soft, cheap. هلکاپن halkā-pan, s. m. Lightness, levity, vanity, despicableness. هلکا جاننا halkā jānnā, To disdain, to disesteem. هلکا کرنا halkā karnā, To lighten, exonerate, to ease, assuage, debase, abase, depreciate, affront.
subs: To be weary (of), to feel burdensome; — to lower or humble oneself
or halkā-phūl, adj. Exceedingly light, light as a feather
v.t. To think lightly or meanly of, to disesteem, despise, disdain, scorn
v.t. To lighten (a burden, &c.), assuage, ease, mitigate, alleviate, exonerate; — to abate, to reduce; — to lower (one, in the esteem of), to disgrace, to debase, to depreciate
(-meṅ), To put a low or moderate price (upon)
wety: Inherited from Sanskrit लघु (laghú).

s.f. A sort of necklace (generally made of rupees, and furnished with a pendant; — cf. haikal).
s.f. formed by imāla fr. ḥamāʼil, q.v.
pron. object. Pl. of mujhe ; Pr. amhe . To us; us.

ety: Ap. Prk. अम्हहिं or अम्हहं, fr. Prk. अहि or अमअ = Vedic S. अस्मे
def: pron. (dat. & acc. of ham, q.v.), To us; us.
more: pron. plur. in the objective case, Us, to us: (or in dakh. nom.) we. ¶ ham-īṅ, We ourselves.

def: adv. contrac. of hamesha, q.v.
adv. Always, ever, continually, perpetually, incessantly
more: adv. Always, ever, continually, perpetually. هميشه کا hamesha kā, Eternal.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian هَمیشَه (hamēša).

def: s.f. Eternity, perpetuity.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian همیشگی (hamēšagī). Equivalent to ہمیشہ (hameśa, “forever”) + ـگی (-gī).

def: adv. Always; incessantly ( sadā ). hameshgī , n. f. Perpetuity; eternity.
s.m. Egotism, vanity, conceit, pride, arrogance.
more: s. m. Vanity, egotism, arrogance, pride.

ety: act. part. n. of حمي 'to protect,' &c.
def: s.m. Protector, defender; supporter, patron; — s.f. Assurance, confirmation or ratification (to an engagement) afforded by a third person's pledging himself to the fulfilment of it
more: n. m. حمي he protected it. A protector; supporter; patron.

(part. act. from حما or حمايت) s. m. A protector, a defender. حامي بهرنا ḥāmī bharnā, To believe, acknowledge, confess, allow, own, consent. Saudā says: نازبرادري کي حامي تب بهرون دل کي کمر دم دلا سادي بندها لون استقامت کي ليي Nāz-bardārī kī ḥāmī tab bharūṅ, dil kī kamar dam-dilāsā de bandhā lūṅ, istiḳāmat ke liye, I will then consent to endure her capricious airs, when by soft persuasion I have fortified my heart for perseverance.
subs: (-kī), To give a pledge or assurance, to pledge oneself (to), to agree, promise, consent, allow, own, acknowledge, confess
s.m. One who gives assurance or satisfaction by pledging himself to the fulfilment of the engagement, &c. entered into by another; — an assurance.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian حَامِی (hāmī), borrowed from Arabic حامٍ (ḥāmin). First attested in c. 1672 as Middle Hindi حامی (ḥamy /⁠hāmī⁠/).

def: n. f. Yes. hāmī bharnā ; Sant. heng , v. n. See iqrār k . 1. To say yes. 2. To confirm.
See حامي بهرنا ḥāmī bharnā, under حامي ḥāmī.

ety: said to be corr. fr. A. ḥāmī, q.v.; but prob. akin to hāṅ, q.v.
def: s.f. Confirmation, ratification (by a third person); assurance, guarantee; assent
subs: (-kī), To afford confirmation (of), to confirm; to pledge oneself (to the fulfilment of); to give assurance (of); to promise, to assent.

def: s.m. One who praises (God).
more: (part. act. of حمد) s. m. A praiser (peculiarly, of God).

ety: ḥamīda, for A. حميدة ḥamīdat, fem. of ḥamīd, fr. حمد 'to praise'
def: adj. Praised, commended, approved; praiseworthy, laudable, commendable, approvable
more: m. (from حمد) adj. 1. Praised. 2. Laudable, glorious. اخلاق حميده aḵẖlāḳ-i-ḥamīda, Laudable qualities or manners. اوصاف حميده auṣāf-i-ḥamīda, Laudable qualifications.
subs: Laudable qualities, praiseworthy manners or disposition

ety: ḥamīda, for A. حميدة ḥamīdat, fem. of ḥamīd, fr. حمد 'to praise'
def: adj. Praised, commended, approved; praiseworthy, laudable, commendable, approvable
more: f. (from حمد) adj. 1. Praised. 2. Laudable, glorious. اخلاق حميده aḵẖlāḳ-i-ḥamīda, Laudable qualities or manners. اوصاف حميده auṣāf-i-ḥamīda, Laudable qualifications.
subs: Laudable qualities, praiseworthy manners or disposition

ety: act. part. n. of حمل 'to bear or carry'
def: s.m. A bearer, carrier, porter; — adj. Pregnant ( = ḥāmila, q.v.)
more: n. m. حمل bore. ( In Comp .) A carrier. hāmil-i-khat, yā ruqqāh . The bearer of a letter or cheque. Nāmah to kaī din se likhā rakkhā thā, lekin Is rashk se kartā na thā qāsid ke havāle, Yeh dīda-i-nā dīdah raheṅ dīd se mahrūm, Aur hāmil-i-khat āṅkh rukh-i-yār pe ḍāle! Jurat. A letter I wrote days ago, but jealousy To send it by a messenger would not let me, To think the bearer of my letter should see thee, While for these longing eyes this pleasure should not be.
(part. act. of حمل Heb. ) 1. s. m. A carrier, porter, bearer. 2. adj. f. Pregnant (a woman). حامل متن ḥāmil-i-matan, A commentary rehearsing, or annexed to, the text.
subs: s.m. A commentary rehearsing, or annexed to, the text.

def: (fr. حمل 'to carry'), s.m. lit. 'What is carried, or carried off or away'; rubbish, rotten leaves or sticks, scum, &c. (carried down by a stream); — a foundling, a bastard, child of a whore; — a surety.
more: (from حمل) s. m. 1. A son of a whore, a bastard. 2. A surety. 3. Herbs, sticks, &c. carried down by the stream.

ety: for A. حاملة ḥāmilat, fem. of ḥāmil
def: v.n. To be or become pregnant, to conceive.
more: adj. حمل carrying. Carrying; pregnant; being with child (H. peṭ se ).
(from حمل) adj. f. Pregnant. حامله هونا ḥāmila honā, To conceive, to be pregnant.

def: adj. & adv. Even this, this very, this same; — equally, in the same manner; — only, solely, neither more nor less.

ety: ham, q.v.+emphat. part. īṅ = ī = hī, q.v.
def: pron. We ourselves; we even, even we.

ety: for A. حامية ḥāmīyat, fem. of ḥāmī
def: s.f. Protectress, &c.; a protective body.
more: s. f. (of حامي) Protectress, female defender.

ety: for A. حمية, inf. n. of حَمِيَ 'to become very hot'
def: s.f. Indignation; scorn; — ardour, impetuosity, zeal; a nice sense of honour; care or concern for what is sacred, or what one is bound to honour or defend.
more: (from حما) s. f. Ardour, zeal, a nice sense of honour.

def: oblique plural of حامی (hāmī, “supporter; backer”)
vocative plural of حامی (hāmī, “supporter; backer”)

ety: act. part. n. of حمض 'to be sour,' &c.
def: adj. Acid, sour, sharp, pungent.
more: (part. act. of حمض Heb. ) adj. Sour.

def: homosexuality
wety: From ہم (ham-, “same, homo-”) + جنس (jins, “sex”) + یت (-iyat, “-ism, -ness, -ality”), likely a calque of English homosexuality.

def: dative/accusative of ہم (ham); plural of مجھ کو (mujh-ko)

ety: inf. n. of حمل 'to bear or carry,' &c.
def: s.m. A burden, load; — gestation, pregnancy; the fruit (of the womb), the young that is borne in the womb; — fruit (of a tree)
more: s. m. 1. A burden, a load. حمل کرنا ḥaml karnā, To load, to impute, to accuse, to ascribe. 2. The fruit (of the womb), pregnancy. 3. Fruit (of any kind). 4. ¶ ḥamal, Aries.
subs: v.t. To load; to ascribe or impute (to), to accuse
v.n. To be or become pregnant.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian حمل (haml), from Arabic حَمْل (ḥaml).

ety: for A. حملة ḥamlat, fem.; v.n. fr. حمل
def: s.m. Charge, attack, assault, onset, storm; aggression, invasion; (fig.) concubitus; — a stroke, beat
more: n. m. 1. Assault; attack; onset; aggression; invasion; assault and battery. 2. Stroke; beat. Ek hamlah kūṭ-kar dūsrā hamlah kūṭ rahā hūṅ . One side is done, I am now doing the other. hamlah-āvar , n. m. An assailant. hamlah khafīf . A petty assault. hamlah k. , v. a. To assault; attack; offer violence. hamle kī dhamkī denā , v. a. To threaten an assault. hamle kī numāish . G. G. Show of assault.
s. m. 1. Attack, onset, assault, storm. 2. ( fig. ) Concubitus. حمله آور ḥamla-āwar, Attacking. حمله کرنا ḥamla karnā, To attack, to assault, to storm, to assail, attempt.
subs: adj. & s.m. Charging, attacking, &c.; — an assailant
(-par), To make an attack (on), to attack, assault, storm, assail; to attempt
ex: رہنما پر حملے کی مذمت کی گئی۔
rahnumā par hamle kī mazammat kī gaī.
The attack on the leader was condemned.
دہشت گرد حملہ
dehśat-gard hamla
terrorist attack
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian حَمْلَه (hamla).

def: direct plural; oblique/vocative singular
oblique plural of حَمْلَہ (hamla, “attack; invasion; assault”)
s.m. Turning (a thing) anxiously in the mind; meditating, purposing; — anxious thought, anxiety, solicitude, grief, care; — purpose, design.
more: s. m. Grief, care, solicitude, turning a thing anxiously in the mind.
ex: ہَم ہون٘گے کامْیاب۔
ham hoṅge kāmyāb.
we will be successful.
ہَم سے نا کَبھی جُدا ہونا۔
ham se nā kabhī judā honā.
Don't ever separate from us.
wety: Inherited from Old Hindi हम (hama), हंम (haṃma), from Apabhramsa अम्हे (amhe), from Prakrit 𑀅𑀫𑁆𑀳𑁂 (amhe), from Sanskrit अस्मे (asme).

ety: v.n. fr. حمل 'to bear or carry'
def: s.m. A carrier of burdens, a porter; a pālkī-bearer.
more: adj. (superl. from حمل) used substantively, A porter, a carrier of burdens.

ety: for A. حمالة ḥammālat, fem. of ḥammāl
def: s.f. A female carrier of burdens.
more: s. f. A female porter or carrier of burdens.

def: s.m. A hot bath; a Turkish bath; a bagnio
more: n. m. حمم became hot. A Turkish bath; a bagnio. Ek hammām meṅ sab naṅge . Prov. One bath, all naked. (We are all in the same boat. Or, We all do the same thing). hammām k. , v. a. To bathe. hammām kī luṅgī , adj. lit . bath sheet. Free to all; common.
(from حمّ Heb. To become warm) s. m. A hot bath, bagnio. ¶ ḥamām, s. A pigeon.
subs: To take a hot bath, to bathe
s.f. 'A bath-sheet'; (hence, a thing) common to all, free.
wety: From Arabic حَمَّام (ḥammām).

def: s.m. The owner or keeper of a ḥammām or bath; an attendant at a bath.
more: n. m. The attendants at a bath.
s. m. A keeper of a bath or an attendant at a bath.

def: s.m. An ass-driver.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian حِمَار (himār), from Arabic حِمَار (ḥimār).

def: compatibility, like-mindedness
ex: مگر اُس سے ہمنوائی نہ تھی۔
magar us se hamnavā'ī na thī.
But we weren't compatible.
wety: From ہم (ham, “us, similar”) + نوائی (navā'ī).

def: n. m. Men of the same profession or calling.
adj. Red, of a red colour.
wety: Borrowed from Arabic حَمْرَاء (ḥamrāʔ), feminine singular of أَحْمَر (ʔaḥmar).

def: adj. & s.m. contrac. of ham-rāh, q.v.s.v. ham.
more: (هم and راه or ره Road) s. m. 1. A fellow-traveller. (Pers. plur. همرهان). 2. adv. With, together with, along with.

def: = P همراهي ham-rāhī, See s.v. هم ham.
more: (هم and راه or ره Road) s. m. 1. A fellow-traveller. (Pers. plur. همرهان). 2. adv. With, together with, along with.

def: = P همراه ham-rāh, See s.v. هم ham.
more: 1. s. f. Fellow-travelling, companionship, company, society in travelling. 2. s. m. f. Fellow-traveller, companion, comrade.

def: fellow traveller; companion; life partner; spouse
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian هَمْسَفَر (hamsafar); can be constructed as ہَم (ham, “also”) + سَفَر (safar, “travel”).

def: neighbor
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian هَمْسَایَه (hamsāya), from Middle Persian [script needed] (hmsʾdk' /⁠hamsāyag⁠/).

ety: prob. ham+tā = tāb or tāw, 'power, dignity,' &c.
def: adj. Equal; like, resembling; — s.m. An equal, a peer; a fellow
subs: adj. Without equal or like, peerless, incomparable.

def: s. f. Likeness, equality.
s.f. Likeness, resemblance; equality, parity.

ety: hām˚ = Zend hãma or hamā = S. सम; ˚ūn or un = wan S. वन्त्
def: s.m. Level ground; a plain; — a desert
more: s. m. A desert, a plain, a level ground.
subs: s.m. Desert-traveller; one who traverses (or has traversed) a desert or deserts.

def: adj. Plain, even, level, smooth; — proportional, symmetrical, well-made; — trained, disciplined; — suitable, worthy, fit
more: adj. Even, level, smooth. هموار کرنا hamwār karnā, To level, smooth; break in, train.
subs: v.t. To level, smooth, make plain; — to break in, to train
adj. Uneven; — unbroken, untrained, undisciplined; — unworthy; indecent; absurd; discordant; — disproportioned; unsuitable.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian هَمْوار.

def: adv. Always, continually, ever.
s.f. Evenness, levelness, flatness, smoothness, &c. (see hamwār).
more: s. f. Evenness, levelness, flatness, smoothness.

ety: for A. همزة hamzat, v.n. fr. همز 'to prick,' &c.
def: s.m. The orthographical sign hamza (ء), or the letter أ alif; — (in accounts) the sign for one-eighth of a ser.

n. m. 1. An orthographical mark (ء). 2. One-eighth of a seer. Hamza bhar ghī, nuqtā bhar āṭā . ( Fac .) A hamza weight of ghī and half a seer of flour. (In accounts the hamza stands for 1/8 of a seer and the dot for half a seer).

n. f. S. हानि See نقصان 1. Loss. 2. Injury; mischief. 3. Deficiency; blank. 4. Slaughter; murder. hān-pūran . An indemnity. hān pūran k. , v. a. To indemnify; reimburse. hān yā ṭoṭā uṭhānā , v. n. To suffer loss.

ety: Prk. थाणे; S. स्थाने, loc. of sthāna
def: adv. To or at the place or house (of; e.g. mere hāṅ, 'to or at my place or house,' Fr. chez moi).
intj. Yes; truly; — make haste! know! have a care! let it not be! far from it! keep off! make way!
s.m. Abandoning, abandonment, relinquishing; — escaping; — prowess, valour.

adv. S. आं Yes; aye. 1. Hāṅ karo yā nāṅh koro, yeh to karnā hī hogā? Say yes or say no, you will have to do it. 2. Phūl na pān, Debī lāṅ hāṅ . Prov. Nor flowers nor leaves on altar lays: 'Yes Goddess, Yes,' is all he says. hāṅ meṅ hāṅ milānā, hāṅ jī hāṅ jī k. , v. n. To say ditto; chime in with. 1. Sab ke sab hāṅ meṅ hāṅ milā rahe haiṅ . All men below seek their own end, with 'yes and ditto' voices blend. 2. Ūṅṭ bilaīyā le gaī to 'hāṅ jī hāṅ jī' kīje . Prov. If one should say the cat took the camel away, 'yes, yes,' you should chime in and say. 3. Tum to hāṅ jī ke naukar ho, 'Hāṅ jī' 'hāṅ jī' kīye jāte ho! 'Yes, Sir,' 'yes, Sir' is then your master, 'Yes, Sir,' 'yes, Sir' you only pratter. hāṅ nāṅ k. , v. a. To say yes and no; say no. Subah hī subah hāṅ nāṅ kartā hai. hāṅ hāṅ k . 1. To say yes. 2. To hum and haw. hāṅ hūṅ, hūṅ hāṅ ; Sant. heṅ hūṅ , n. f. Hum and haw. hāṅ hūṅ k. , To hum and haw.

ety: i.q. hūṅ, 'I am,' dialec. hauṅ, Panj. hāṅ
def: intj. Yes, aye; indeed, verily; by the by, forsooth
more: s. f. Loss, deficiency, harm, injury, slaughter.
interj. Make haste! know! have a care! let it not be! far from it.
1. adv. Yes, ay, indeed, by the by, forsooth. هان کہنا hāṅ kahna, To agree. 2. (contract. of يہان yahāṅ, q. v.) Here, hither, &c.
subs: Yes, Sir; oh, assuredly!; — s.f. Servile or obsequious assent (to everything), obsequiousness
or hāṅ-jī hāṅ-jī karnā, To say 'yes' (to everything), to approve and assent to with obsequiousness or adulation
To say 'yes,' to assent, agree, to yield
adv. Yes, yes; yes to be sure, &c.
or hāṅ-hūṅ (or hūṅ-hāṅ) karnā, To say 'yes, yes'; — to hum and haw
adv. (emphatic) Yes, yes indeed, yea verily.

def: adv. contrac. of yahāṅ, q.v.
perf. part. of next, q.v.
adj. & s.m. Killing, slaying; — killer, slayer, slaughterer, murderer (used as last member of compounds).
s.m. Striking; wounding; hurting, injuring; destroying; killing, slaying; — slaughter; destruction; — (in Arith.) multiplication
subs: adj. Of a cruel or murderons disposition cruel; murderous
(-ko), To wound; to kill, slay; to destroy.

def: (colloq.), s.m. per met. for nahān = snān, qq.v. s.m. Bathing, ablution; purification, lustration
s.f. = halas = haris, qq.v. s.f. The beam of a plough (i.q. haris).
part. Stricken; injured; slain.

ety: Prk. भंडो, or भंडओ; S. भाण्डः, or भाण्डकः
def: s.m. A (large) cooking-pot (whether of earth or metal), a cauldron.

n. m. S. हण्डा 1. A large earthen pot (بهاندَا). 2. A grass found on the banks of tanks and jhīls . It produces a little red flower. hanḍā k. , 1. To offer many earthen pots full of sweetmeats to a god. 2. To give a great feast after one's return from a pilgrimage.
s.m. A large earthen pot or boiler, a cauldron; — a species of grass (found on the banks of tanks and jhīls, and producing a little red flower)
more: s. m. A caldron, an earthen pot or boiler. هنڐا پهوڙنا hanḍā phoṛnā, v. a. ( lit. to break the pot) To disclose a secret, to let the cat out of the bag.
subs: 'To break the pot' (of); to disclose a secret, to let the cat out of the bag
(-kā), To offer earthen pots (full of sweetmeats, &c., to a god); to give a great feast (esp. on returning from a pilgrimage).

ety: Prk. भंडो, or भंडओ; S. भाण्डः, or भाण्डकः
def: s.m. A (large) cooking-pot (whether of earth or metal), a cauldron.
v. a. See ﮩـاندَنا

v.t. To cause to roam or to wander; to send or drive forth or out; to expel, banish; to disgrace by public exposure, &c. (as by taking one round a city on an ass); — to take or move round (syn. ghumānā).
more: v. a. 1. To banish, to expel, to drive out of a city, to disgrace (by publick punishment). 2. To move round.

ety: for A. هندسة handasat, the P. andāza arabicized
def: s.m. Geometry; — figure, or figures (of arithmetic); — arithmetic
more: (dakh. hindsa, from Pers. اندازه) s. m. Geometry, figures, arithmetick. هندسه دان handasa-dān, Geometrician, arithmetician, accountant.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian هَنْدَسَه (handasa), from Arabic هَنْدَسَة (handasa), itself a Middle Persian borrowing. Doublet of اَنْدَازَہ (andāza).

ety: Prk. भंडिआ; S. भाण्डिका, whence हण्डिका
def: s.f. A small earthen boiler or pot (i.q. hāṅḍī); — a relish
more: s. f. A small earthen pot (for boiling meat, &c.).
subs: 'The pot to boil'; (meat, &c.) to be cooked; (a scheme, &c.) to be concocted
v.n. The pot to be put on (the fire); to earn; — to take bribes; — to fill one's pockets
'The pot to be hot or boiling'; to earn; to be making money fast; to take bribes
s.f. Pots and pans, cooking utensils.
wety: Inherited from Sanskrit हुण्डिका (huṇḍikā). Cognate with Punjabi ਹੁੰਡੀ (huṇḍī) / ہُنْڈِی (huṇḍī), Sindhi 𑋞𑋣𑋟𑋈𑋢 (huṇḍī) / हुंडी (huṇḍī) / هُنڊِي (huṇḍī), Assamese হুণ্ডী (hundi), Bengali হুন্ডি (hunḍi), Odia ହୁଣ୍ଡି (huṇḍi), Gujarati હૂંડી (hū̃ḍī), Marathi हुंडी (huṇḍī).

def: s.f. An earthen pot (for cooking, &c), a small cauldron;-globe (of a lamp)
more: s. f. A pot, a small caldron.
subs: A pot to boil over; — (fig.) to swell out
The pot to boil; — to talk, to gossip; — a plot or plan to be concocted or matured
To break an earthen pot (over the name of anyone; this is done on the departure of an obnoxious individual)
(-par), The pot to be put (on the fire)
'The pot to become warm or to be busy' (esp. by fraudulent means); to pilfer, to pocket, &c
wety: Inherited from Middle Hindi هانڐي (hanḍy) (c. 1621), from Sanskrit हण्ड (haṇḍa). Cognate with Malvi हाँड़ी (hā̃ṛī), Bengali হাঁড়ী (hā̃ṛī), Marathi हंडा (haṇḍā).

def: n. f. S. हण्डी 1. An earthen cooking pot. 1. Jo kuchh hāṅḍī meṅ hogā, so rakābī meṅ āvegā . Prov. What's in the pot will come on the plate. 2. Bharī hāṅḍī jhīkḍā baḍerī lāge dhūāṅ . Bhoj. Prov. A pot full of shards, and such a smoke! (Great boast, little roast). 3. Jākī hanḍlī vā-kī manḍlī . Rus. Prov. Who owns the pot has friends a lot. 2. The globe of a lamp. hāṅḍī ubalnā , v. n. 1. To boil over (a pot). 2. To swell out. hāṅḍī paknā , v. n. 1. To boil (the pot). 2. To talk; gossip. 3. To be concocted or matured (a plan, plot). hāṅdī phoṛnā, yā kisī ke nām par hāṅdī phoṛnā , Tir. lit . to break an earthen pot. This is done on the departure of an obnoxious individual whose exit is looked upon as auspicious. O jekhan jaitā tekhani hunkā noūṅ se pachās hāṅḍī phūṭat . Tir. hāṅḍī chaṛhnā , v. n. To be put on the fire (the pot). hāṅḍī garm h. , v. n. To bag; pocket. hāṅḍī-vāl , n. m. A partner. bāolī yā dīvānī hāṅḍī , n. f. Hodge podge.

n. f. S. हङ्कार Call; cry ( pukār , 1). 1. Gūṅge ne ke māre hāṅk, āṅdhe ne ke dīje jhāṅk? Rus. Prov. Who heard the dumb man call, who saw the blind man peep? (The fool will never learn). 2. Ān gāoṅ āgi lāge, ān gāoṅ dhuāṅ, Koirī ke ḍār jāre, hāṅk pāre kuān . Riddle. Ans. A Huqqa. A village is on fire, another village smokes away, The Koeri's branch aburning is, the well begins to bray. hāṅk pukār , n. f. A hue and cry; hullabaloo. hāṅk pukār-ke kahnā , To say publicly so as to be heard by all. hāṅk mārnā , v. a. To call or bawl after ( pukārnā 3). hāṅkā hāṅk , n. m. Driving (an animal).

Now curb your tongue! why, goodness me! to whom did I e'er go? Come shut up there! and dont invent and go on lying so! 5. To ask an exorbitant price. Stretched on his bed all day he brags and boasts away.

n. m. S. हंस 1. A goose; a swan. 1. Kulāh hans kī kavvā khaṛā utāre hai, Uchhal-ke meṅḍkī hāthī ke lāt māre hai . The times out of joint. Nazīr.
